metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Sayam Khatri — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The core number, 28,800 companies, is impractical to fully process given API costs and limits. Roughly 40 percent of target companies have no discoverable website to scrape at all. The dataset records that companies haven't sponsored but never explains why. Absence of sponsorship evidence is not evidence that sponsorship is impossible.

artifactLines: The core number, 28,800 companies, is impractical to fully process given API costs and limits.
Roughly 40 percent of target companies have no discoverable website to scrape at all.
The dataset records that companies haven't sponsored but never explains why.
Absence of sponsorship evidence is not evidence that sponsorship is impossible.

beats.narration_text: The line that absence of evidence is not evidence of absence, applied to companies that simply haven't been asked to sponsor, reframed the whole 28,800 number for me. I want to reason about it rigorously. Help me: (1) explain why the dataset can show non-sponsorship but never the reason, (2) work through how API cost and limits make scraping 30K companies impractical, and (3) design one signal that could distinguish a company that won't sponsor from one never asked.
